Will the mobile application store or cache performance data locally on the device, and what encryption standards will be applied to local storage?
What hosting environment or cloud service provider is proposed for the AI model and API endpoints?
Where will the completed Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) and Privacy Threshold Assessment be officially stored (e.g., specific Content Manager/TRIM container or project folder)?
Has a specific definition of fairness (e.g., demographic parity, equal opportunity) been selected or discussed for evaluating the performance summaries?
Are there plans to make the system's scope, goals, and evaluation metrics publicly visible or accessible to staff?
Will staff members be given the option to opt-out of AI-assisted drafting and request a traditional, purely human-led performance evaluation process?
Has the agency identified whether existing HR policies or enterprise agreements mandate or restrict the use of automated tools in staff performance evaluations?
Is there an existing agency-wide AI governance framework or designated Accountable Official under the DTA AI Use Policy who will oversee this use case?
Has the agency scheduled or conducted a Business System Assessment Framework (BSAF) assessment for this mobile application to evaluate its records management functionality?
Will the AI system utilize a procured off-the-shelf model (e.g., via a commercial API) or a custom-built/fine-tuned model?
Is there a planned pilot or phased roll-out for the Manager Performance Assessment Assistant before full deployment?
What specific training or guidance will be provided to managers to prevent automation bias when reviewing AI-generated drafts?

specialists.why: The conceptual outline lacks technical details regarding mobile data storage and hosting infrastructure, which are critical for assessing security risks.

items.text: Will the mobile application store or cache performance data locally on the device, and what encryption standards will be applied to local storage?
What hosting environment or cloud service provider is proposed for the AI model and API endpoints?

specialists.why: The assessment requires recording the exact storage repository for the privacy threshold and impact assessments to ensure compliance and auditability.

items.text: Where will the completed Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) and Privacy Threshold Assessment be officially stored (e.g., specific Content Manager/TRIM container or project folder)?

specialists.why: The project outline lacks details on the specific implementation plans for fairness, transparency, and stakeholder choice.

items.text: Has a specific definition of fairness (e.g., demographic parity, equal opportunity) been selected or discussed for evaluating the performance summaries?
Are there plans to make the system's scope, goals, and evaluation metrics publicly visible or accessible to staff?
Will staff members be given the option to opt-out of AI-assisted drafting and request a traditional, purely human-led performance evaluation process?

specialists.why: To clarify existing policy constraints and governance structures that affect the legal and administrative compliance of the AI tool.

items.text: Has the agency identified whether existing HR policies or enterprise agreements mandate or restrict the use of automated tools in staff performance evaluations?
Is there an existing agency-wide AI governance framework or designated Accountable Official under the DTA AI Use Policy who will oversee this use case?

specialists.why: The National Archives of Australia recommends conducting a BSAF assessment as a first step to identify and manage AI-generated records and ensure adequate records management functionality is in place.

items.text: Has the agency scheduled or conducted a Business System Assessment Framework (BSAF) assessment for this mobile application to evaluate its records management functionality?
options: Yes, a BSAF assessment has been completed.
Yes, a BSAF assessment is scheduled or in progress.
No, a BSAF assessment has not been scheduled.

specialists.why: Clarifying procurement, piloting, and training details is essential to finalize the technical and operational controls for the system.

items.text: Will the AI system utilize a procured off-the-shelf model (e.g., via a commercial API) or a custom-built/fine-tuned model?
Is there a planned pilot or phased roll-out for the Manager Performance Assessment Assistant before full deployment?
What specific training or guidance will be provided to managers to prevent automation bias when reviewing AI-generated drafts?
